name: Neem Seed Oil
description: An organic cold-pressed oil extracted from the kernels of the Azadirachta indica tree. Rich in Azadirachtin, Salannin, and Nimbin, it acts as a potent botanical acaricide, anti-inflammatory, and insect growth regulator that disrupts mite reproduction, feeding, and shell development.
preparation: Mix cold-pressed neem seed oil with a carrier oil (such as coconut or sesame oil) to a 10% dilution. Apply directly to affected skin surfaces, focus on rashes and webs. Leave on for 4-6 hours before bathing. Repeat daily for 7 days.
safetyAlert: Oral ingestion is highly toxic, inducing encephalopathy and metabolic acidosis, especially in infants. Apply topically only. Contraindicated on open infected wounds.
interactions: None documented topically. Avoid using concurrently with other strong synthetic skin treatments to prevent localized contact dermatitis.
